title: Tactics: X-Ray Attack
summary: Attack through pieces - the hidden X-Ray attack.
description: An X-Ray attack (or see-through attack) occurs when a piece attacks an enemy piece through another piece. The attacking piece doesn't actually capture anything on the way - it's the threat on the piece behind that matters. This is a sophisticated GM-level tactical concept.
objectives: Recognize when pieces are lined up through each other
Understand the difference between X-Ray and discovered attacks
Master the concept of attacking through pieces
plans: Look for pieces that can attack through other pieces
Often X-Ray attacks target the king or queen
The piece in between might be able to move, creating discovered attacks
commonMistakes: Don't confuse X-Ray with discovered attacks - they're different
Remember: the piece in between doesn't block the attack
X-Ray attacks can be very subtle
completionSummary: You've learned to use X-Ray attacks to create hidden tactical threats.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Recapture
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black moves the bishop to b6.

steps.title: X-Ray attack setup
steps.instruction: Play Bg5! This attacks the knight on f6 through the queen on d8!
steps.coaching: This is an X-Ray attack! The bishop attacks f6 through the queen on d8. When the bishop moves, it might reveal an attack on the queen!
steps.hint: Move the bishop to g5 - an X-Ray attack!

steps.title: Black defends
steps.instruction: Black plays h6, defending the bishop.
steps.coaching: Black defends the bishop, but we can now attack through it!

steps.title: Capture on f6
steps.instruction: Capture on f6! The X-Ray attack succeeded!
